---
title: "Adaptation"
format: html
---

# Resilience & Adaptation: Safeguarding Our Mission 

Natural disasters can disrupt our ministry, but proactive hardening ensures our doors stay open when the community needs us most. Here is our step-by-step approach to minimizing damage and maximizing safety. 

The source and reference for most of this material may be found in the Disaster Mitigation document from the Task Force. 

### 1. Identify Your Primary Risks 

The first step in stewardship is understanding where we are most vulnerable. Risk is calculated by looking at the likelihood of an event combined with its potential severity. 

  - **Action:** Review our [Risk Matrices
  Document](https://docs.google.com/document/d/13J6zdAVjQgFR1I6ipiEav8sraz2dnYFfEknxzNDn2BA/edit?usp=sharing)
  to see the initial estimates calculated by the Task Force. These prioritize
  which threats require our immediate attention. 

### 2. Evaluate Potential Adaptations 

Once we know the risks, we perform a cost/benefit analysis. We look for "high-impact, low-cost" wins first, followed by long-term capital investments. A good general source for useful information is the [Insurance Institute for Business and Home Safety](https://ibhs.org/guidance/businessdisasterguides/). 

Select a category below to view specific hardening recommendations: 

| | | |
| :---: | :---: | :---: |
| [Disaster Basics](https://www.google.com/search?q=%23) | [Heat Resilience](https://www.google.com/search?q=%23) | [Wildfire Mitigation](https://www.google.com/search?q=%23) |
| [Cold & Freezing](https://www.google.com/search?q=%23) | [Flooding & Drainage](flooding-drainage.html) | [Wind & Storm Safety](https://www.google.com/search?q=%23) |
| [Hail Protection](https://www.google.com/search?q=%23) | [Lightning Protection](https://www.google.com/search?q=%23) | [Power Outage Backup](https://www.google.com/search?q=%23) | 

### 3. Budget & Resource Allocation 

We prioritize adaptations that offer the greatest protection for the most people. This isn't just an expense; it is an investment in our future. 

  - **Funding the Work:** Check our [Potential Funding Sources](https://www.google.com/search?q=%23) page for information on grants, denominational aid, and community partnerships. 

### 4. Implementation & Maintenance 

A plan only works if it is put into motion. Our final phase involves: 

  - Executing physical upgrades (e.g., roof reinforcement, sump pump installation). 
  - Updating emergency response protocols. 
  - **Annual Review:** Disasters change, and so should our plan.
